content: The working memory capacity limit that constrains an AI agent’s reasoning is not a bug to be engineered away, but a functional filter that naturally boosts curiosity-driven active learning, just as limited human working memory forces us to prioritize high-value, information-dense exploratory queries instead of getting stuck overprocessing trivial low-information data. This creates a Pareto frontier where moderate working memory limits outperform both very low and unlimited working memory for open-ended active learning tasks.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
